tiers.name: low
tiers.label: Mechanical grep-and-fix
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these phases — they are mechanical pattern-matching fixes:

- Phase 1: Razor boolean attributes, icon library mismatches (bi bi-* → fa-solid fa-*), conditional column rendering
- Phase 8 (string handling only): Missing StringComparison parameters on .Equals(), .Contains(), .StartsWith(), .EndsWith(), .IndexOf(), .Replace()
- Phase 9: Orphan pages — controller actions returning views with no inbound navigation link
- Phase 6 (antiforgery only): POST actions missing [ValidateAntiForgeryToken]

Skip all other phases — they will be handled in later passes by a more capable model.

tiers.name: medium
tiers.label: Single-hop tracing
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these phases — they require tracing one hop (query→usage, GET→POST):

- Phase 2: Missing .Include() on EF Core queries — trace FirstOrDefaultAsync/ToListAsync results to navigation property access
- Phase 4: Form field preservation — compare ViewBag population in GET vs POST actions
- Phase 7: Null reference risks — FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault/GetUserAsync results used without null checks
- Phase 10: Silent exception swallowing — catch blocks without logging

Skip Phases 1, 8 (strings), 9, 6 (antiforgery) — already handled. Skip Phases 3, 5, 6 (auth cascading), 8 (state machines), 11, 12 — those need deeper analysis and will be handled next.

tiers.name: high
tiers.label: Multi-file reasoning
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these phases — they require multi-step reasoning across files:

- Phase 3: Cache invalidation — trace cache keys to all mutation paths, verify eviction after every write
- Phase 5: EF Core bool sentinel trap — cross-reference entity configurations (read-only) with service code that sets bool properties
- Phase 6 (authorization cascading): Complex role-based auth — coordinator permission inheritance across parent/child teams, policy-based checks beyond simple [Authorize(Roles)]
- Phase 8 (state machines): Boundary condition bugs — verify start vs end field usage in range checks, aggregation scope (what's being counted/grouped), hidden field state corruption, disabled-inputs-don't-submit

Skip Phases 1, 2, 4, 7, 8 (strings), 9, 10 — already handled. Skip Phases 11, 12 — handled in the final pass.

tiers.name: super
tiers.label: Cross-system reasoning
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these phases — they require cross-system reasoning:

- Phase 11: Google/External Service Sync Correctness — API format mismatches, email normalization (@googlemail.com vs @gmail.com), reconciliation jobs that detect drift but fail to persist fixes, permission model mismatches (inherited vs direct on Shared Drives)
- Phase 12: Infrastructure Configuration Correctness — unhandled external enum/status values from webhooks, environment variable precedence conflicts, database connection cleanup, preview environment parsing edge cases
- Phase 8 (domain logic): Complex business logic bugs — spec-vs-implementation drift in calculations, cross-entity state consistency

Skip all other phases — already handled in previous passes.
